query: advantages of glass ionomer cement reinforced with metal particles

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Metal Modified GIC
retrieved_docs.content: Metal particles in the silver alloy are added to the conventional GIC powder to improve the mechanical properties of GIC and permeate its use in Class I cavities in molar teeth. The GIC can be reinforced with metal in a silver admixture or cermet mixture.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Mechanical properties such as wear resistance, strength, and fracture toughness are superior to conventional GIC but inferior to composite resins. Compomer bonds micro-mechanically with tooth structure but has no chemical bonding ability. Fluoride release is reduced compared to conventional GIC, but esthetics are superior.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties of Metal Modified GIC
retrieved_docs.content: The wear resistance is substantially improved in metal-modified GIC compared to conventional GIC. The wear resistance of cermet is greater than that of silver alloy admixed GIC. The compressive strength and fracture resistance are mildly improved. The use of metal-modified GIC in high-stress-bearing areas is restricted to primary dentition only.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Advantages
retrieved_docs.content: Chemical bonding to the tooth structure [5] Caries preventive action [8] Thermal compatibility with tooth structure Mild pulp response [5] Tooth-colored restorative material [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Dispersed phase glasses: using dispersed phases of strengthening crystallites like aluminum oxide, titanium oxide, and zirconium oxide improved the strength. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- Glass Ionomer Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages: Improved adherence (via dentin bonding) Increased resistance to root fracture Enhanced resistance to solubility in tissue fluids Antimicrobial properties Sustained fluoride release [16] [17] Disadvantages: Technically difficult to prepare deep aspects of root canal (potentially limits dentin-bonding ability) Difficult to remove in cases of endodontic retreatment [16] [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Cermet ionomer cement: manufactured by sintering the metal and glass powders together, which helps achieve a strong metal bond to the glass. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Restorative material: Thanks to its easy placement and better marginal adaptation, glass ionomer cement is popularly used in pediatric restorations. GIC is also indicated for restoring permanent teeth in low stress-bearing areas like class III and V lesions and is the material of choice in patients with a high risk of caries due to the release of fluoride. Luting agent: GIC can lute indirect restorations (metal and metal-ceramic), post, core, and orthodontic bands, and brackets. [4] [8] Pulp protection: GIC is used as a liner or base beneath metallic and composite restorations (sandwich technique). [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Fiber-reinforced glasses: adding alumina fibers, glass fiber, silica fiber, and carbon fiber to the glass ionomer powder increased flexural strength. [17] [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Disadvantages
retrieved_docs.content: Poor mechanical properties, eg, low compressive strength, low abrasion resistance, and fracture resistance, make its use restricted to low stress-bearing areas [5] [7] Poor esthetics due to lack of translucency Moisture sensitive while setting [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: Metal reinforced GIC: the mixture of amalgam powder and GIC, commonly known as 'miracle mix.' [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties of Metal Modified GIC
retrieved_docs.content: Chemical bonding to the tooth structure is slightly lessened due to the silver present in the composition. Both silver alloys admixed GIC and cermet release fluoride in significant quantities. However, the fluoride release is comparatively less in cermet than in silver alloy admixed GIC. Metal-modified GICs have improved handling properties and exhibit fewer porosities in set cement.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Second Generation
retrieved_docs.content: In this type of cement, the polyacid was already added to the powder, and the setting was achieved by mixing the powder with water or a tartaric acid solution. This GIC is known as water-hardening cement. The main advantages of the second-generation system are enhanced shelf life, decreased initial viscosity, and improved strength.

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Equipment -- Hybrid sealants
retrieved_docs.content: Hybrid sealants represent a newer category and include materials such as compomers (polyacid-modified composite resins) and giomers. Compomers combine elements of both composites and glass ionomers, including reactive fillers and photo-initiators, while giomers contain pre-reacted glass ionomer (PRG) particles within a resin matrix. [20] Although these materials aim to merge the benefits of both parent systems, they are not yet widely used in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Silver Alloy Admixed GIC
retrieved_docs.content: The powder of this modification of GIC is obtained by adding 1 part of spherical silver alloy particles to 8 parts of conventional GIC powder. The liquid consists of polyacrylic acid. The powder and liquid are mixed at a ratio of 3:2 by weight. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2039
retrieved_docs.content: Iron in other combinations is classified as an iron antianemic preparation. Iron antianemic preparations are therapeutic agents used to replenish iron stores and treat iron‑deficiency anemia. Formulations described as “iron in other combinations” contain iron together with additional compounds or nutrients, enhancing absorption or providing complementary benefits while still serving the primary purpose of correcting iron deficiency. Recognizing these combination products as a subset of iron antianemic preparations helps clinicians select appropriate therapy for patients with anemia, especially when co‑existing deficiencies or specific tolerability issues are present.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 1752
retrieved_docs.content: Magnesium gluconate is contraindicated in patients with قصور الكلى (renal failure), meaning its use should be avoided in individuals suffering from kidney failure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2587
retrieved_docs.content: Bismuth subnitrate and bismuth subcitrate are therapeutic agents that may be used to treat الإِسْهال (diarrhea) and اضطراب مَعِدِيّ (gastrointestinal disturbance). Both compounds share the same clinical indications, offering similar benefits for managing these gastrointestinal conditions. However, each is contraindicated in the presence of الاعتلال الخثري (renal impairment), meaning that patients with renal disease should not receive either bismuth subnitrate or bismuth subcitrate. Recognizing the parallel efficacy and shared contraindication of these bismuth salts is essential for safe prescribing and optimal patient care.
